creator: karels@freebsd.org
text:
I tested realtek-re-kmod-199.00_1 from pkg with a kernel from a day or two ago.  It still crashes, but I learned a little more.  Running single user, I ran ping, ssh, and nfs client (all using IPv4, I realized afterwards) without problems.  Then I went multi-user, and the system crashed with an mbuf modified after free.  This was about the time that mrouted started, and I see multicast operations in the backtrace:

Starting nfsuserpanic: Memory modified after free 0xfffff8014db96700 (256, mbuf) + 8 = deadc0dedead4d73

cpuid = 0
time = 1706114192
KDB: stack backtrace:
db_trace_self_wrapper() at db_trace_self_wrapper+0x2b/frame 0xfffffe022baac780
vpanic() at vpanic+0x131/frame 0xfffffe022baac8b0
panic() at panic+0x43/frame 0xfffffe022baac910
trash_ctor() at trash_ctor+0x74/frame 0xfffffe022baac920
item_ctor() at item_ctor+0x5b/frame 0xfffffe022baac970
mld_v2_enqueue_group_record() at mld_v2_enqueue_group_record+0x6d0/frame 0xfffffe022baaca40
mld_change_state() at mld_change_state+0x5ed/frame 0xfffffe022baacab0
in6_joingroup_locked() at in6_joingroup_locked+0x3ae/frame 0xfffffe022baacb70
ip6_setmoptions() at ip6_setmoptions+0xe06/frame 0xfffffe022baacd20
sosetopt() at sosetopt+0xc2/frame 0xfffffe022baacd80
kern_setsockopt() at kern_setsockopt+0xa5/frame 0xfffffe022baacde0
sys_setsockopt() at sys_setsockopt+0x24/frame 0xfffffe022baace00
amd64_syscall() at amd64_syscall+0x153/frame 0xfffffe022baacf30
fast_syscall_common() at fast_syscall_common+0xf8/frame 0xfffffe022baacf30
--- syscall (105, FreeBSD ELF64, setsockopt), rip = 0x31d805e30bea, rsp = 0x31d802ab0938, rbp = 0x31d802ab09d0 ---
KDB: enter: panic

I have the coredump and core.txt; let me know if you want anything.
